channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Daveigh Chase's Property Left Without Will; Mother Files Petition in LA
item.description: The article discusses a legal dispute involving Daveigh Chase, an American actress known for her role in the TV series 'Teen Wolf,' who passed away without leaving a will. Her mother has filed a petit...

item.title: Karnataka High Court slams West Bengal over delayed citizenship verification of detained school bus driver
item.description: The Karnataka High Court on Monday criticised the West Bengal government for requesting additional time to verify the citizenship documents of Rafikul Biswas, a school bus driver detained in Bengaluru...

item.title: KeNHA to Close Coptic Roundabout for 30 Days for Reconstruction
item.description: The Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has announced a temporary closure of the Coptic Roundabout on the Mamboleo-Kiboswa (A1) Road in Kisumu County. The partial closure, set to start on July 2...

item.title: Mofokeng Reflects on Gratitude and New Chapter as He Leaves Orlando Pirates
item.description: Relebohile Mofokeng, the young star who has joined Belgian club Royale Union Saint-Gilloise, penned an emotional farewell message to Orlando Pirates. In a six-page post on his social media, Mofokeng e...

item.title: Nigeria's former tribunal chief faces corruption charges after acquitting Tinubu
item.description: Danladi Umar, the former chairman of Nigeria's Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT), is facing corruption charges and has been remanded in prison in Abuja. This marks a significant shift for a man who previ...

item.title: Cotabato City gunman arrested after ambush killing
item.description: A 29-year-old gunman, Jamir Diduanda Sailon, was arrested in Cotabato City after ambushing a tricycle driver and wounding two others. The incident occurred in Barangay Poblacion 5 on July 12, 2026. Sa...

item.title: High-ranking criminal arrested in Essendon for involvement in Melbourne bar conflicts and arson
item.description: The article details the arrest of a prominent individual linked to violent clashes between rival gangs in Essendon, Victoria. The person is suspected of orchestrating a series of arson attacks targeti...

item.title: Family SUV Chased and Attacked in Nashik Molesting Incident
item.description: A 44-year-old woman tourist was allegedly molested and her family members attacked by a mob armed with iron rods and baseball bats at Bhavali Dam in Nashik district on Sunday. The incident occurred wh...

item.title: Venezuela's Oil Production Remains Unaffected by Earthquakes, President Says
item.description: Venezuela's acting president, Delcy Rodriguez, has assured that the country's oil production is not impacted by recent earthquakes that killed over 4,500 people. Despite the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quak...

item.title: Pessamit Innu Community Marks Quebec's Rejection of Agreement
item.description: The Pessamit Innu community in Quebec has expressed joy over the rejection of a proposed agreement with the provincial government, which they believe undermines their sovereignty and cultural rights. ...

item.title: Controversy Over Trump's Motorcade Damage Claims at Reflecting Pool
item.description: President Donald Trump drained the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, claiming vandals had caused a 300-yard gash in its lining. However, photos and reports from onlookers revealed tire tracks left by ...

item.title: African leaders collaborate on border governance and migration challenges
item.description: King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has emphasized the need for enhanced cooperation among African nations to address illegal immigration and border governance during a high-level meeting with Zimbabwean Presi...

item.title: Windsor Judge Dismisses $13M Drug Smuggling Charges Over Charter Rights Violations
item.description: A Windsor judge has dismissed $13 million drug smuggling charges against cross-border truck driver Harinder Singh Saini, citing 'egregious' Charter rights violations by Canada Border Services Agency (...

item.title: Critique of Government Report on Antifeminist Ideologies and Gender Issues
item.description: The article critiques a Canadian government report titled 'Confronting Antifeminist Ideologies in Canada,' which focuses on online trends like 'looksmaxxing' (a term associated with the 'manosphere' a...

item.title: Vybz Kartel and Sidem Face Rumors of Split After New Woman Appears in Video
item.description: Vybz Kartel, the Jamaican dancehall star, has sparked rumors of a split with his long-time fiancée Sidem after posting a video of a casual outdoor gathering. The clip shows him at a relaxed event with...

item.title: Yanga delays Mwamnyeto contract renewal over financial terms
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) are delaying the renewal of Bakari Mwamnyeto's contract due to financial considerations. The defender, who joined Yanga from Coastal Union in 2020, has become a key player in th...

item.title: Railway linen theft costs contractors ₹104 crore; 56% rise in thefts
item.description: An RTI-based investigation by The Indian Express reveals that between January 2022 and May 2026, at least 1.27 crore bedroll items were stolen from Indian Railways trains, causing a loss of ₹104.51 cr...

item.title: FIFA Removes French Referees from 2026 World Cup Roster Amid Speculation
item.description: FIFA has removed two prominent French referees, François Letexier and Clément Turpin, from the 2026 World Cup roster. While initial speculation linked their exclusion to a controversial match between ...

item.title: Tanzania Faces Human Rights Concerns Over President Samia's Remarks
item.description: Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan faces criticism from human rights groups and opposition parties over recent statements that are perceived as undermining civil liberties. The controversy stems ...

item.title: Two Shabu Dealers Arrested in Maguindanao del Norte with Family Assistance
item.description: Two individuals involved in selling shabu near school areas were arrested in Maguindanao del Norte after their relatives and local leaders facilitated the operation. The suspects, Bairuth Paches and A...
